content:
# Q: [Antigravity] App crashes on OAuth login: Uncaught TypeError: Do not know how to serialize a BigInt
**Source:** https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/issues/23857

## The Problem / Request
### What happened?

Hello team,

I encountered a critical crash in the Antigravity desktop client during the OAuth login flow on Windows. I managed to identify the root cause and implemented a temporary workaround, but this requires a proper fix in the core codebase.

### Description
When authenticating the application, the browser successfully completes the authorization and redirects back to the app via the `antigravity://oauth-success/` protocol. However, the application fails to process the callback. The main process throws an uncaught `TypeError` because the underlying code uses `JSON.stringify()` on a payload or state object that contains a `BigInt` value.

### Environment
* **OS:** Windows 10/11 (win32-x64)
* **Version/Commit:** `135ccf460c67c4b900dc10aa71c978f27d78601c`

### Steps to Reproduce
1. Launch Antigravity and trigger the sign-in flow.
2. Complete the OAuth verification in the browser.
3. The browser attempts to redirect to the app (`antigravity://oauth-success/...`).
4. The application window becomes unresponsive to the login success, and the logs reveal a main process crash.

### Error Logs
```text
[main 2026-03-26T03:52:40.540Z] app#handleProtocolUrl(): antigravity://oauth-success/ { originalUrl: 'antigravity://oauth-success/' }
[main 2026-03-26T03:52:40.540Z] URLHandlerRouter#routeCall() with URI argument antigravity://oauth-success/
[main 2026-03-26T03:52:41.671Z] [uncaught exception in main]: TypeError: Do not know how to serialize a BigInt
[main 2026-03-26T03:52:41.671Z] TypeError: Do not know how to serialize a BigInt
    at JSON.stringify (<anonymous>)
    at vD (file:///D:/Antigravity/resources/app/out/main.js:31:11765)
    at nve.o (file:///D:/Antigravity/resources/app/out/main.js:31:14636)
    at nve.m (file:///D:/Antigravity/resources/app/o

us>)
    at vD (file:///D:/Antigravity/resources/app/out/main.js:31:11765)
    at nve.o (file:///D:/Antigravity/resources/app/out/main.js:31:14636)
    at nve.m (file:///D:/Antigravity/resources/app/out/main.js:31:14511)
Workaround / Suggested Fix
I was able to bypass the crash and successfully log in by injecting the following polyfill at the very beginning of resources/app/out/main.js:
BigInt.prototype.toJSON = function() { return this.toString(); };
To permanently fix this, please either implement a replacer function in the JSON.stringify calls that handle the auth payload/storage, or apply a similar polyfill globally.

Thank you!

### What did you expect to happen?

After completing the OAuth authorization in the browser, I expected the Antigravity application to successfully handle the antigravity://oauth-success/ protocol redirect, parse the authentication payload without crashing, and complete the login process so I could access the IDE.

### Client information

<details>
<summary>Client Information</summary>

由于是 Antigravity 桌面端客户端崩溃，以下为提取的客户端信息：

```text
App: Antigravity IDE (VS Code-based)
Target Platform: win32-x64
OS: Windows_NT x64 (Windows 10/11)
Installation Path: D:\Antigravity
Commit Hash: 135ccf460c67c4b900dc10aa71c978f27d78601c

</details>

### Login information

Standard browser-based OAuth login flow triggering the antigravity:// deep link. No proxy or custom network configuration was used.

### Anything else we need to know?

I was able to temporarily bypass this crash and log in successfully by injecting a polyfill (BigInt.prototype.toJSON = function() { return this.toString(); };) at the very beginning of resources/app/out/main.js.

The crash clearly originates from a JSON.stringify() call attempting to serialize an auth state or token payload that includes a BigInt value without a proper replacer function. Fixing this at the core serialization utility level should resolve the issue.

## The Solution / Discussion
### Response 1
Found possible

at includes a BigInt value without a proper replacer function. Fixing this at the core serialization utility level should resolve the issue.

## The Solution / Discussion
### Response 1
Found possible duplicate issues:

- #23856

If you believe this is not a duplicate, please remove the `status/possible-duplicate` label.
<!-- gemini-cli-deduplication -->

### Response 2
This repository is for Gemini CLI, not Antigravity.

You will have to go through Antigravity folks for feedback and support. https://antigravity.google/support
